name: Tom Sancton
abstract: Thomas Alexander Sancton is an American writer, jazz clarinetist and educator. From 1992 to 2001 he was Paris bureau chief for TIME Magazine, where he worked for 22 years, and he has contributed to numerous publications including Vanity Fair, Fortune, Newsweek and the Wall Street Journal.

concerts.label: Preservation Hall Jazz Band and Friends
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Dorothy Tait
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] You see a boy like young Tom Sancton, who played at George's Wake... [INTERVIEWEE] And we might say here that, uh, Tom Sancton is from New Orleans, which is quite unusual.

about_quotes.speaker: Bernard Steinau
